source.title: Hedonic hot spot in nucleus accumbens shell: where do mu-opioids cause increased hedonic impact of sweetness?

confidence_rationale: Primary, anatomically precise demonstration that a circumscribed cubic-millimetre region of NAc medial shell (the 'hedonic hot spot') is sufficient to amplify orofacial liking reactions to sucrose when activated by the mu-opioid agonist DAMGO. Outside the hot spot, DAMGO infusions in the rest of medial shell produced increases in food intake (wanting) WITHOUT increases in liking reactions -- a within-shell anatomical dissociation of wanting and liking substrates. This directly supports the architectural premise of MECH-295 that liking has its own dedicated substrate (tag b: liking encoding). It does not prove the necessity link between liking and approach (the experiment increases liking pharmacologically; it does not block liking and observe approach failure), but it establishes that liking is encoded by a specific, separable circuit -- a prerequisite for any 'liking is required intermediate' claim. Confidence 0.78: high source quality, direct anatomical evidence, but the experiment increases rather than ablates liking, so it is one logical step removed from the necessity claim.
failure_signatures: If liking and wanting were generated by the same neural population, mu-opioid stimulation of the hot spot should increase both equivalently; the paper shows the spatial distribution differs.
If REE's liking-stream were a passive readout of drive level, no dedicated anatomical substrate should exist; this paper shows one does.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Microinjection of the mu-opioid agonist DAMGO into a small (~1 mm cubed) sub-region of the rostrodorsal medial shell of NAc selectively amplifies positive hedonic 'liking' orofacial reactions to sucrose. DAMGO injections elsewhere in medial shell increased food intake (wanting) without increasing liking. The hot spot defines an anatomically discrete generator of hedonic impact.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-295 posits a liking-stream as the necessary intermediate between drive amplification (SD-012) and approach-cue selection. For this architecture to be biologically plausible, the liking-stream must have its own substrate that is dissociable from the wanting/incentive-salience substrate. Pecina & Berridge 2005 provide that prerequisite: the NAc shell hedonic hot spot is the biological correlate of MECH-295's liking-stream activation node. The fact that the wanting-amplifying remainder of medial shell does NOT amplify liking shows that drive-amplified eating can occur without liking activation -- which is consistent with the EXQ-483 signature (override fires, drive raised, but approach_commit = 0.0). The paper is the strongest available anatomical grounding for the substrate of MECH-295's liking node.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper amplifies liking and observes increased liking; it does not block liking and observe whether wanting/approach also fail. Therefore it does not arbitrate between (a) liking as a NECESSARY intermediate (MECH-295 strong reading) and (b) liking as a PARALLEL signal that augments approach but is not strictly required. The latter is the standard wanting/liking-can-dissociate reading. The strong necessity claim of MECH-295 requires hot-spot lesion or mu-opioid-antagonist studies showing that drive-amplified approach to a learned cue collapses without liking activation. That experiment is not Pecina 2005.
mapping.source_context: Adult Sprague-Dawley rats; bilateral cannulation of medial NAc shell; DAMGO microinjection in 23 sub-sites; orofacial taste-reactivity scoring (positive: tongue protrusions, lateral tongue protrusions, paw licks; aversive: gapes, headshakes); within-subject design with vehicle controls; Fos mapping confirmed local activation.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality high (J Neurosci, primary data, anatomically precise, replicated by the same group in subsequent decade). Mapping fidelity moderate-high: the paper substantiates that liking has its own substrate (which MECH-295 requires) but does not directly test the necessity link. Transfer risk moderate -- rat orofacial reactivity is the canonical liking measure but mapping it to an artificial agent's approach_commit metric requires interpretive bridge.
